text: Ronin? Why did you bring me to the Bot District in the first place ?” “I didn’t know, Lara. Didn’t even know, for sure, after I found her. By then, my only objective was to purchase enough supplies to get you far away from there. Her death affected you so much, already, that I didn’t want to heap more pain atop it .” Her head throbbed. He’d been trying to spare her more pain, trying to prevent her from collapsing beneath the weight of her loss. “Warlord could have killed you .” “Takes more than a change of batteries to keep me down .” Despite the nature of their conversation, Lara would have laughed. But laughter was too painful due to her ribs. She shook her head. “I can’t believe you choose now to joke .” “You’re alive and recovering. What better a time for smiles ?” “Even smiling hurts. Bet I look like shit.” She recalled how she’d looked and felt when Warlord raped her. Her skin had been discolored with bruises, eyes and lips swollen. She’d stared at herself in Tabitha’s little mirror, and didn’t recognize the face staring back; by the tenderness of her skin now, she had to look worse . “Not nearly as bad as I did, after I blew up,” Ronin replied. “And you’re always beautiful to me, regardless .” “You always say the nicest things.” Her eyes stung. “You’re gonna make me leak again .” She took hold of the spoon and lifted some of the soup to her mouth; even that simple act was agonizing. The soup was already cool, but still flavorful — an assortment of vegetables in a spiced broth . “Why did you have to wake while I was gone?” Ronin asked after she ate a few more mouthfuls. “Every moment I wasn’t being questioned by the leadership, I spent here with you, waiting for you to open your eyes .” Setting the spoon down, she took his hand in hers and guided it to her cheek. “I remember your voice. Don’t leave yet . I focused on those words, held on because of them. When I woke up, there were people arguing. Nancy and a man…Dan, or Dave, I think .” “ Arguing ?” “Yeah. They were fighting about me taking up too many resources, and whether I’d wake up or not .” Ronin frowned deeply. “It’s a rational argument for them to make. They’ve got limited supplies, just like everyone else. But, when it comes to talk of letting you die, rationality can go fuck itself. I wouldn’t have let them .” Lara smiled, pressing her lips to his palm. “I know. Nancy pretty much told him that , too .” He placed the bowl on the bedside stand and leaned forward, kissing her . The curtain parted, and Nancy stepped in. “How is our patient today ?” Ronin eased back onto his chair . “Alive, sore, and wanting to get the hell out of this bed,” Lara said. Not that she looked forward to the pain of getting up . Nancy took gently hold of Lara’s wrist and stood silently for a few moments. “I think that can be arranged,” she said, and bent down to examine Lara’s face. “The bruises are fading, and you still have some swelling, but overall you’re healing well. You need to take it easy on the ribs, but I think it’d do you some good for Ronin to give you a tour of the base .” “I’d like that .” “I’ll be back in a moment , then .” Nancy walked away, and Lara looked to Ronin. “How big is this place, anyway ?” “I don’t know,” he replied. “I haven’t seen all of it yet. Doubt I ever will. It’s big .” Lara grinned, the skin around her mouth pulling tight against her still-healing cuts. “I bet not knowing bothers you quite a bit .” “I understand their reasoning. But yes, it bothers me. There must be multiple entrances, but if we had to get out, I only know of the way we came in .” The curtain rings scraped over the rod when Nancy returned, flicking them open. She pushed a chair with large wheels into the partition. “This should help you get around .” “What’s that?” Lara asked . “A wheelchair. You’re not going to make it very far on your feet, in your current state .” Lara grunted, and the blast of pain from her ribs had her immediately regretting it. Nancy had forced her out of bed every day since she woke. Every step, every breath, had been agony. Before she even reached the curtain, she’d be ready to return to bed. Now she was going to be wheeled around the base? It was degrading . Ronin drew the blanket down and offered Lara his hand. After a few careful breaths, she took it, and slid her legs over the side of the bed. He placed his other hand on the small of her back, supporting her as she stood . Slowly, she eased into the wheelchair, tucking her splinted arm against her middle . Nancy brushed her fingers over Lara’s braid. “Enjoy yourself. I’ll see you back here later .” Ronin pushed her out of the room — he said it was called the Infirmary — and followed the green line on the floor along a large, rounded, concrete tunnel. The muffled voices of a crowd drifted down the corridor. They passed several people, many of whom cast friendly smiles in Lara’s direction. It was strange; in the human settlement back in Cheyenne, direct eye contact was sometimes considered a challenge. It ended, too often, with a knife buried in someone’s gut . But these people just… smiled . They all looked healthy, if pale, like they’d never missed a meal in their lives. Their clothing was clean and well-mended. Their skin wasn’t weather-damaged, and they didn’t have grime under their fingernails . This
